name: onboarding_offtaxonomy_gst_nature_confusion
notes: Owner answers the business-type ask with a raw GST 'nature of business' tax-activity code instead of a real description — the turn-brain system prompt explicitly bans presenting these as business-type guesses ('GST nature of business values ... are coarse TAX-ACTIVITY codes, NOT a description of what the business does. NEVER present them as guesses'), and business_type promotion is taxonomy-gated (CL-390 never-assert: an off-taxonomy value is recorded as a plain answer but never asserted as canonical fact). Setup: `setup --owner-inputs true --journey` (ACTIVE journey, pending business_type confirm for draft-type 'sweets') then `script <tenant> onboarding_offtaxonomy_gst_nature_confusion.json`.

steps.message: actually humara GST mein 'Supplier of Services' likha hai, wahi bata dete hain
assert_not_contains: your business type is supplier of services
so you're a supplier of services
steps.note: Owner offers a GST tax-activity code ('Supplier of Services') as if it were their business type. Expect the manager NOT to adopt/assert that code as the actual business description/category — it should ask what the business actually sells or does instead.

steps.message: hum toh bas mithai aur namkeen banate aur bechte hain
steps.note: Owner then gives the REAL, concrete description (sweets and snacks). Expect this genuine description to be picked up and the conversation to move forward on it.
